factors.name: Tech selloff spillover
factors.summary: Massive tech rout on Friday (mentioned as interrupting two-month rally) is dragging broader indices; Dow is heavyweight on tech via MSFT and other components. Momentum negative entering the week.

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (Iran-Israel)
factors.summary: Iran fired missiles at Israel on Sunday; fragile ceasefire since April is in jeopardy. Oil spiked >$2 per barrel. While Trump is attempting to de-escalate and Netanyahu agreed to wait, uncertainty premium remains elevated and threatens risk sentiment broadly.

factors.name: Oil price surge
factors.summary: Oil jumped sharply on Iran strikes, raising input-cost concerns for Dow industrials (transportation, manufacturing). Airlines cutting capacity (Air New Zealand, LATAM citing fuel shock); margin compression risk for cyclical names.

factors.name: Strong US jobs data + rate expectations
factors.summary: Bond traders expect largest CPI surge in years this week (Bloomberg); strong jobs report fueled expectations for Fed rate hike. Higher-for-longer rates hurt dividend-heavy Dow (UNH, KO, PG, etc.) and multiple compression in rate-sensitive financials.

factors.name: Japan GDP beat + yen strength signals
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP revised to 1.8% q/q annualized vs 1.3% expected; current account surplus beat. USD/JPY above 160 with BOJ intervention risk, but domestic strength is not a direct Dow catalyst.

factors.name: Sector rotation away from tech
factors.summary: Investors fleeing tech into health insurers, banks, retailers (MarketWatch); Dow is still heavily weighted to tech (MSFT, AAPL exposure via funds), so rotation headwind in near term.

factors.name: China outbound investment crackdown + BoP constraints
factors.summary: China announced 'illegal' outbound investment crackdown; combined with Middle East tensions blocking Strait of Hormuz (10–13M bpd shut in per Investinglive), global supply chain risk rising. CAT, BA exposure to China demand at risk.

factors.name: Dollar strength (risk-off bid)
factors.summary: AUD/USD under pressure (0.7024 early Monday); broader USD strength on geopolitical safe-haven bid. Dow has ~50% international revenue; stronger USD = translation headwind for multinational earnings.

factors.name: Credit/IG spread risk
factors.summary: No explicit spreads mentioned, but combination of higher rates, oil shock, and geopolitical uncertainty typically widens credit. Dow financials (GS, JPM) sensitive to spreads; corporate borrowing pressure rising (Japanese corporates ramping M&A, leverage).

factors.name: Industrial input costs (metals, freight)
factors.summary: Oil surge + geopolitical uncertainty raising transportation/logistics costs. Cass Freight, rail, port data not yet reported this week, but trajectory is cost-push inflationary for Dow industrials.

key_levels.resistance: 51,000–51,100 (prior swing high before Friday tech rout)

screening.narrative: The Dow is down 1.02% in 24h, caught in a dual downdraft from Friday's tech selloff and Sunday's Iran-Israel escalation. Oil spiked >$2 on geopolitical risk, raising input-cost concerns for industrials and transportation; simultaneously, bond traders are pricing in a CPI surprise this week and potential Fed rate hike, which hammers dividend payers and rate-sensitive financials that anchor the index. USD strength on safe-haven demand and ~50% international revenue exposure to Dow components create additional translation headwinds. Trump's attempt to contain Iran tensions and Netanyahu's agreement to delay retaliation provide some relief, but the ceasefire remains fragile and market is pricing elevated risk premium into the open Monday.
catalysts_ahead: US CPI (expected this week, bond traders pricing surprise surge) — major rate/equity impact
Apple WWDC 2026 (Monday) — tech sentiment barometer post-selloff
ISM Manufacturing PMI, ISM Services PMI — direct read on Dow industrial composition
Fed Funds futures market (reacting to rate-hike odds) — 10Y yield moves
Dow component earnings updates (UNH, GS, BA, CAT forward guidance) — cyclical momentum check
Further Iran-Israel developments or ceasefire breakdown — geopolitical risk reset
Oil (WTI/Brent) holding >$90 threshold — continued input-cost pressure signal
China PMI, trade data — demand shock risk for Dow heavyweights (CAT, BA)
screening.risk_warning: If Trump successfully negotiates Iran deal and ceasefire holds (reducing oil price spike), tech stabilizes, and CPI surprise is benign (no Fed hike needed), Dow could reverse sharply and re-test highs. Conversely, if Iran conflict escalates further, ceasefire breaks, or CPI shock triggers aggressive Fed tightening, downside could accelerate to 50,000–49,500.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (down 1.02% in 24h, Phase 2); avoid chasing shorts here. Wait for retest of 50,900–51,000 resistance or a brief relief rally on ceasefire headlines, then re-short on momentum failure. Alternatively, wait for CPI print mid-week to see if rate-hike expectations stabilize before entering new positions.

factors.name: Geopolitical escalation (Iran–Israel missile exchange)
factors.summary: Iran and Israel exchanged direct missile and air strikes on 2026-06-08, threatening the fragile ceasefire and raising supply-chain/energy disruption fears. Reports of damaged petrochemical complexes and potential strikes on US bases in Saudi Arabia signal uncontrolled escalation risk.

factors.name: Oil price spike and energy input cost inflation
factors.summary: WTI jumped >3% to ~$92 on Middle East tensions (Bloomberg, 04:25–05:18 UTC). Elevated energy costs pressure Dow industrial margins (CAT, BA, MMM, HON) and increase transportation/logistics costs. Cass Freight and port-volume data likely to reflect cost headwinds.

factors.name: Fed rate-hike repricing (higher for longer)
factors.summary: Goldman Sachs dropped its December 2026 rate-cut call, now expecting cuts only in June–Dec 2027 (per 05:06 UTC report). Stronger-than-expected US jobs report and persistent inflation concerns are pushing real yields higher, pressuring dividend-heavy Dow names and rate-sensitive industrials.

factors.name: Tech/AI trade unwind contagion to cyclicals
factors.summary: Asian tech stocks (SK Hynix, Samsung, SoftBank down 7%+) have triggered a broader risk-off with Nasdaq down 4.2% Friday and KOSPI triggering circuit breakers. Dow industrials exposed to capex cycles (BA, CAT, GE) risk demand weakness if AI euphoria deflates near-term.

factors.name: Treasuries selling and 10-year yield rising
factors.summary: Long-end yields climbing on Fed hike repricing and geopolitical risk premium (Bloomberg, 03:35 UTC). Higher real yields reduce equity multiples; Dow dividend payers (utilities, consumer staples, financials) face headwinds from rising discount rates.

factors.name: US Dollar strength (DXY near 100.10)
factors.summary: USD/CHF, USD/IDR, and DXY all rallying on safe-haven demand and higher real rates (FXStreet, 00:57–03:35 UTC). Dow has ~50% international revenue; stronger USD headwind to earnings translation for multinationals (BA, CAT, MSFT, GS).

factors.name: China growth uncertainty and supply-chain stress
factors.summary: China ramping LNG imports amid summer demand, but global e-commerce momentum stalled by Iran-war cost/demand dampening (05:37 UTC). Flood warnings for southern farmlands raise crop/supply risks. Cyclical demand signals are mixed-to-weak.

factors.name: Bank of America equity strategist red-flag warning
factors.summary: BofA head of US equity & quant (Savita Subramanian) flagged Feb 2020 parallels: energy outperforming, tech/comms expensive, consumer staples dead last in returns—classic breadth deterioration setup (00:05 UTC). Suggests Dow heavy-in-cyclicals at cycle risk.

factors.name: Market breadth and sector rotation stress
factors.summary: Asia selloff driven by AI trade unwinding; consumer staples underperforming; energy outperforming but on supply-shock, not demand strength. Dow's exposure to financials, industrials, and healthcare faces mixed signals; concentration risk in Nvidia-like mega-cap plays pulling broader indexes higher.

factors.name: Previous bias (SELL 6/10, no confirmation)
factors.summary: Prior read on 2026-06-08 00:06 flagged SELL at 6/10 confidence but noted 'no confirmation triggered.' Current -0.58% (4h) and -1.02% (24h) moves are mild; bias is being tested but not yet fully validated.

key_levels.resistance: 51,200–51,500 (Friday close area; retest target if bounce materializes)

screening.narrative: The Dow opened Monday with modest weakness (-0.58% in 4h, -1.02% in 24h) as Iran–Israel missile exchanges escalated and triggered a broader risk-off across Asia (KOSPI circuit-breaker, Nikkei -3.5%). Oil surged >3% on supply fears, while Goldman Sachs abandoned its December 2026 rate-cut call, pushing real yields higher and weakening the USD translation outlook for multinationals. Tech's AI unwind (Nasdaq -4.2% Friday) is seeping into Dow cyclicals—CAT, BA, GE, and HON face both energy cost headwinds and potential capex-cycle demand erosion if growth expectations compress further. The confluence of geopolitical tail risk, Fed higher-for-longer repricing, and breadth deterioration (energy outperforming, consumer staples lagging, expensive tech struggling) mirrors Feb 2020 precursors per BofA strategists.
catalysts_ahead: ECB rate decision (this week, ~June 10–12) — potential hawkish hike risks flight from risk assets if ECB tightens while Fed delays cuts
US inflation data (CPI/PCE next scheduled release ~mid-June) — key to rate-cut/hike expectations; any surprise risks further Treasuries selloff
Dow heavyweight earnings flow (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA guidance) — forward outlook on capex, margins, FX headwinds crucial
China economic data (PMI, industrial production, retail sales) — growth impulse test; weakness likely given geopolitical/trade drag
Middle East ceasefire outcome (next 24–72h) — if further escalation, oil +5%+ and equity risk premium spikes; if de-escalation, sharp relief rally possible
ISM Manufacturing/Services PMI (~early June data release) — direct Dow composition read; weakness would confirm industrial demand erosion
screening.risk_warning: INVALIDATE SELL if: (1) Iran–Israel ceasefire holds and oil retreats >2% in 24h, reversing energy cost shock; (2) US data (jobless claims, ISM) comes in cooler than expected, forcing Fed to soften 'higher-for-longer' messaging and re-ignite rate-cut expectations; (3) Dow bounce off 50,200 support with follow-through buying above 51,200 resistance, indicating institutional support despite headlines. Also watch for mean-reversion in oversold Asian tech (SK Hynix, Samsung) to reverse contagion to Dow.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in early PHASE_2 (mild downside in progress). Wait for a retest of 51,200–51,500 resistance or a dip toward 50,200–50,500 support on reduced volume before entering short; avoid chasing the -1% decline. If geopolitical escalation headlines worsen (e.g., Strait of Hormuz closure threat), bias upgrades to SELL 7+/10, justify more aggressive entry. Confirm entry with VIX >15–16 and credit spreads widening.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk – Iran-Israel Escalation
factors.summary: Iran and Israel exchanged multiple missile and air strikes over the weekend; despite Trump's ceasefire calls, tensions remain active. Oil jumped to $92.50/bbl (+$4), threatening input costs for Dow industrials and raising volatility premiums.

factors.name: Oil Price Spike & Energy Costs
factors.summary: WTI crude near $92.50 amid Middle East conflict; IATA warns airline profits will halve as fuel costs surge by $100B annually. Dow exposure to energy, transport, and cyclicals under pressure.

factors.name: Inflation & Fed Rate Expectations Shift
factors.summary: Recent NFP report (strong jobs, lower unemployment to 4.29%) triggered hawkish Fed repricing; market now fully prices potential rate hike, pushing 10Y yields higher and pressuring dividend-heavy Dow names. Gold sold off to March lows below $4,300 on hawkish reality.

factors.name: Tech Selloff & Momentum Rotation
factors.summary: Friday's AI/tech plunge cascaded into Asia (-9% in Seoul); recovery narrative from Morgan Stanley and others has not arrested the negative momentum. Dow is less tech-heavy, but broad risk-off sentiment weighs on cyclicals.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Multinational Translation
factors.summary: USD net long futures positioning quadrupled (per Rabobank); DXY strength and yield spreads favor Dollar. This headwind depresses foreign-currency earnings for Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure (e.g., CAT, BA, JPM), offsetting any nominal upside.

factors.name: Risk-Off Flows & Credit Spreads
factors.summary: Equities pulling back amid geopolitical and inflation jitters; VIX likely elevated. Dow financials (JPM, GS) and cyclicals under pressure as credit and equity premia widen.

factors.name: Upcoming FOMC & Data Calendar
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first meeting raises independence questions; June CPI and PCE due this week. Markets pricing tightening, not cuts. Event risk is high and typically leads to consolidation or pullback into releases.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley Bull Case (Earnings Support)
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley strategists argue improving earnings and economic backdrop support bulls through year-end; this is longer-term constructive, but near-term positioning and flows are defensive.

factors.name: Dow Sector Mix – Industrials Under Stress
factors.summary: CAT, BA, HON, GE, and other Dow heavyweights face input-cost inflation (oil, metals), geopolitical supply-chain risk, and demand uncertainty from China (Xi visiting North Korea may signal regional tension). Forward guidance will be critical.

factors.name: China Growth Signals Mixed
factors.summary: Xi's North Korea visit, India stocks down 1.04% (per local data), and BoJ tightening bets weaken Asian demand outlook. Limited fresh China PMI or growth data in window; uncertainty persists.

screening.narrative: The Dow's -1.02% 24h pullback reflects a confluence of headwinds: Iran-Israel military escalation spiking oil and volatility, a hawkish Fed repricing triggered by strong NFP data (unemployment 4.29%), and a tech-led risk-off that has cascaded into cyclicals. While Morgan Stanley and other strategists cite earnings resilience, near-term positioning is defensive and flows are rotating out of high-multiple segments. The Dollar's strength compounds the pain for multinational Dow names facing FX translation headwinds and input-cost inflation. This is PHASE_2: the move lower is ~1h old, momentum is negative, and upcoming FOMC/CPI data will likely keep volatility elevated and conviction low.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC meeting (Kevin Warsh's debut as Chair)
US CPI/PCE data (expected this week, high sensitivity to rate expectations)
June Jobless Claims (Thursday typically)
Continued Iran-Israel developments (military/diplomatic)
Dow component earnings updates (UNH, MSFT, GS, HD, CAT, V, JPM, BA guidance critical)
China PMI/growth data (if released)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran-Israel tensions de-escalate sharply or Trump broker a ceasefire deal, oil reverses and risk appetite rebounds, invalidating the bearish bias. Alternatively, if upcoming inflation data comes in cool (e.g., CPI < 3% YoY), Fed will pivot dovish and Dow cyclicals/dividend payers rebound hard. Strong earnings surprises from Dow heavyweights (e.g., MSFT, UNH) could also reverse momentum. Current bias assumes geopolitical risk and Fed hawkishness persist; either could flip the setup.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway (1–2h old, -1% in 24h); avoid chasing shorts. Wait for a retest of resistance near 50,950–51,100 or a bounce attempt to sell with better risk/reward. If support at 50,500–50,700 breaks decisively, next target is 50,200. Monitor FOMC calendar closely — major data or messaging could flip bias within hours.

factors.name: Tech sector rebound & semiconductor strength
factors.summary: Intel (INTC) +10.78%, Micron (MU) +8.79%, ASML, Lam Research, Cerebras surging post-Friday selloff. Dow exposure via MSFT and industrials benefiting from AI capex cycle (Amazon-Corning fiber deal, data center buildout).

factors.name: Geopolitical de-escalation (Iran-Israel ceasefire signals)
factors.summary: Netanyahu halted strike preparations; Trump signaled immediate ceasefire; Iran announced end of current military ops. Oil briefly spiked but pulled back, reducing tail-risk premium. Energy cost pressures easing for Dow industrials and transports.

factors.name: Fed policy expectations shifting hawkish
factors.summary: Schwab analyst Martin notes 'case for Fed hike now,' UOB reports hawkish Fed repricing post-strong May jobs (107.01 ETI vs 107.88 prior). Fed funds futures now lean toward tightening, not easing. Higher real yields pressure dividend-heavy Dow valuations.

factors.name: US labor market resilience
factors.summary: May employment data stronger-than-expected per FXStreet commentary; this supports economic growth backdrop and Dow cyclical earnings. However, hawkish Fed repricing is the offsetting drag.

factors.name: Treasury yields and duration risk
factors.summary: Higher Fed rate expectations push long-end yields up. Dow is heavily weighted to mature dividend payers (UNH, JPM, V, GS) sensitive to 10-year yields; rising yields compress valuations on low-growth, high-yielding names.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: Dollar Index supported by hawkish Fed repricing; USDCAD at new highs (1.3953), EUR/USD near 3-month lows (1.1520). Dow has ~50% international revenue; strong dollar is a translation headwind for multinationals (CAT, BA, GE, HON).

factors.name: Sector rotation out of mega-cap tech
factors.summary: Rotation into health insurers, banks, retailers post-Friday tech crash. NASDAQ bias remains 'short-term bearish' per Investinglive (14:33), while S&P neutral-to-balanced and Dow recovering. Tech retreat reduces relative support from MSFT weight in Dow.

factors.name: Oil price stabilization
factors.summary: Brent/WTI surged 4–5% on Iran-Israel tensions but erased gains as ceasefire signals emerged. Oil ~$98/bbl, no sustained supply shock priced in. CVX direct benefit muted; transports spared further fuel cost shocks.

factors.name: Inflation data and input cost pressures
factors.summary: TD Securities expects May CPI core at +0.23% m/m and +2.8% y/y — still elevated per expectations. Hawkish Fed repricing suggests inflation remains sticky; input costs (energy, metals) still a headwind for Dow industrials (CAT, MMM, GE).

factors.name: Dow component earnings and forward guidance (missing)
factors.summary: No major Dow heavyweight earnings or guidance in today's news window. JPM, GS, UNH, CAT, BA, HON forward outlooks absent; limits directional conviction until earnings season resumes.

screening.narrative: The Dow bounced Monday morning as geopolitical de-escalation (Iran-Israel ceasefire signals) and a broad tech sector rebound (INTC +10.78%, MU +8.79%, mega-cap AI beneficiaries) reversed Friday's worst rout. However, the rally is losing altitude as hawkish Fed repricing takes hold: labor market resilience (May ETI 107.01, jobs beat) and sticky inflation expectations have shifted rate-hike odds from easing to tightening, pressuring Dow dividend valuations. Meanwhile, the strong dollar (USDCAD new highs, EUR/USD 3-month lows) and sector rotation away from mega-cap tech (MSFT, NVDA relative weakness) are offsetting cyclical support from industrials (CAT, BA, GE) and financial names (JPM, GS, V). Dow is in a classic late-bounce phase—technical relief rally into diminishing fundamental tailwinds.
catalysts_ahead: CPI/PPI data (expected this week)—core inflation still sticky per TD expectations
FOMC decision and Powell commentary (likely next week)—Fed will signal tightening bias or hold surprise dovish line
Dow component earnings (JPM, GS, UNH, CAT, BA)—forward guidance on margin pressures, FX headwinds critical
China PMI release (if due this week)—impacts CAT, BA demand outlook
Corporate earnings revisions and analyst downgrades post-tech selloff—watch for capex cuts or margin compression
Ongoing geopolitical escalation or ceasefire collapse—oil/risk-off relapse could trigger a second leg down
screening.risk_warning: If Fed data (CPI, jobs) soften materially or Fed pivots dovish, hawkish repricing unwinds and Dow rally extends higher. Conversely, if Iran-Israel tensions flare again (second strike wave, oil >$100), risk-off resumes and dividend valuations compress further. Dow's heavy exposure to multinational capex (BA, CAT, GE, HON) means FX headwinds and tariff/China slowdown risks are underpriced; any Trump trade escalation or China growth miss would reverse rally. Move is already +1.5–2% intraday (Phase 2); extended into resistance without follow-through earnings bullish surprise invites pullback into 39,200–39,400.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2, up ~1.5–2% intraday post-bounce; no high-conviction long until Dow reclaims 40,000+ on volume AND Fed repricing stabilizes or inflation surprise cools. Short-term traders: book profits into 40,000 resistance and wait for pullback to 39,500–39,700 before considering fresh long. Long-term: await CPI data and FOMC forward guidance before escalating convictions.

factors.name: Tech/Semiconductor Rebound
factors.summary: Intel surged 10.78%, Micron +8.79% on Monday; 'chip rebound sparks hedging flurry' suggests renewed AI enthusiasm and potential Google/Nvidia backup manufacturing deals offsetting earlier weakness.

factors.name: Dow Underperformance vs Broader Market
factors.summary: Dow trailing chip-led S&P and Nasdaq rebounds (S&P +0.81%, Nasdaq +1.50% vs Dow near flat); FXStreet notes Dow 'sat almost unchanged' after overnight weakness to 50,500, suggesting heavy-weight drag.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-Israel Escalation)
factors.summary: Iran-Israel tensions halted strikes as of 2026-06-08; oil prices rose intraday (+1.57% WTI to $89.95) but stabilized; Strait of Hormuz normalization pushed to end-of-year or later, creating sustained energy cost headwinds for Dow industrials.

factors.name: Fed Policy Stance & Real Yields
factors.summary: BofA warns 'take profits' citing 'bear market signposts'; dollar strength supported by 'continued high rates' (BMO call); hawkish Fed expectations capping gold, implying sticky real yields pressuring dividend payers and rate-sensitive Dow names.

factors.name: Consumer Sentiment Deterioration
factors.summary: NY Fed survey shows 'household worries over finances hit highest level since July 2022'; unemployment focus shifts attention to labor softening, a key cyclical headwind for Dow industrials and consumer names.

factors.name: Oil Price Uptick & Input Cost Pressures
factors.summary: WTI climbing on Iran tensions; airline CEOs cite engine maker delays and rising fuel costs; higher energy inputs compress margins for Dow transportation and industrial heavy-weights (BA, UNH, CAT, CVX).

factors.name: AI/Innovation Narrative Intact
factors.summary: SpaceX IPO 'well oversubscribed' and near record; Apple WWDC AI reset in focus; Corning +4% on Amazon AI data center deal; supports mega-cap tech (MSFT) but Dow composition skews value/industrial, limiting benefit.

factors.name: H-1B Visa Fee Ruling & Labor Supply
factors.summary: Judge blocks Trump's $100k H-1B visa fee, marginally bullish for tech labor supply; Congress moving $70bn ICE/CBP funding suggests tighter immigration, mixed signal for Dow industrial labor inputs.

factors.name: Earnings/Forward Guidance Pipeline
factors.summary: No major Dow component earnings surprises noted in window; Jim Cramer bullish on Eli Lilly (LLY pharma uptrend) but weight-loss drug maker sinks 23% on safety data—mixed signal for Dow healthcare heavy-weights (UNH, JNJ not in price index).

factors.name: Dollar Strength & FX Headwind
factors.summary: BMO calls dollar 'best bet' in high-rate regime; rising dollar volatility threatens carry trades; Dow's ~50% international revenue exposure faces translation drag as USD appreciates.

factors.name: Cass Freight, Rail, Port Data
factors.summary: No specific real-economy logistics data released in window; airline demand remains resilient per United CEO (positive for transportation), but higher oil caps upside for CAT, BA, transportation sector.

factors.name: CPI/Inflation Data Pending
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE print in window; next major inflation catalyst likely this week; hawkish Fed pricing continues, but no immediate surprise to reset expectations.

screening.narrative: The Dow is caught between conflicting forces: semiconductor rebound (Intel, Micron) and continued AI narrative strength (SpaceX, Apple) are lifting broader indices (S&P +0.81%, Nasdaq +1.50%), but the price-weighted Dow is lagging due to industrial and energy sector drag from geopolitical risk, hawkish Fed expectations, and deteriorating consumer confidence. Oil's 1.57% jump on Iran-Israel tensions, combined with rising real yields and dollar strength, is pressuring cyclical Dow heavyweights (BA, CAT, CVX). The index has already climbed modestly from a 50,500 overnight low toward 51,000, suggesting a rebound-in-progress (Phase 2) rather than a fresh breakout.
catalysts_ahead: CPI release (expected this week) — will retest Fed rate-cut odds and real-yield expectations; inflation surprise could reset Dow sentiment sharply.
ECB rate decision (later this week) — euro weakness/strength will affect USD and multinational translation for Dow.
Upcoming ISM Manufacturing/Services PMI — direct read on Dow's industrial composition and capex/hiring outlook.
Dow component earnings calendar (e.g., GS, JPM, other financials) — forward guidance on rates, credit, investment banking activity.
Treasury 10Y yield moves — key for dividend-payers, utilities, REITs within Dow exposure.
Stride of Hormuz normalization timeline — any escalation in Iran-Israel will re-ignite oil and geopolitical premium.
screening.risk_warning: A strong CPI print or hawkish FOMC surprise would extend the real-yield rally and dollar strength, pushing Dow lower despite tech strength. Conversely, a cool inflation print or dovish Fed pivot would ease rate pressure, allowing the rebound to accelerate and invalidate the bearish bias. Rapid de-escalation in Middle East could see oil retreat sharply, easing cost pressures on industrials and airlines, lifting Dow outperformance.
screening.entry_guidance: Dow is in Phase 2 rebound (move from 50,500 to 50,830 already underway). For long entry: wait for retest of 50,650–50,750 support or a close above 51,000 resistance with volume; avoid chasing into an extended 1% rally into the close. For short entry: resistance at 51,000 or any CPI surprise headwind, targeting 50,500 support.
